steps.title: Translate the input x = 6
steps.content: In $P(x) = 547(1.010)^x$, $x$ is the number of years after 1952. So $x = 6$ corresponds to the year $1952 + 6 = 1958$.

steps.title: Translate the output P(6) ≈ 581
steps.content: $P(x)$ is the modeled population. Therefore $P(6) \approx 581$ says the population in 1958 is approximately 581.

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A says the population grew by about 6 from 1952 to 1958, confusing the input $x = 6$ with the output.
  • B says the population is about 581 in 1958 — this matches the interpretation of $P(6) \approx 581$.
  • C says the 1958 population is 6 times the 1952 population, but $P(0) = 547$ and $581/547 \approx 1.06$, not 6.
  • D describes a constant increase of 581 every 6 years, but the model is exponential, not linear, and 581 is a population value, not an increment.
B is correct.
